commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=610c503e77b01ef259a16f3b09447672d939fc1b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `rfcs/0009-iroh-gateway-transport.md`, a draft RFC proposing an optional experimental Iroh Gateway transport with new `gateway.iroh.*` config, pairing payload, and security/audit considerations.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a draft RFC for a new optional Gateway transport, not a current behavior bug. The merge issue is source-verifiable by comparing this PR with the open RFC 0009 proposal at https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/8.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide numbering, RFC discussion, and transport direction; there is no narrow automated repair that should land without that review.

target.title: RFC 0009: Iroh Gateway transport
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Iroh as an optional, experimental OpenClaw Gateway transport so clients can pair and reconnect without requiring a VPN such as Tailscale.

## Review focus

- Whether the first implementation should use native Gateway protocol framing over Iroh QUIC streams or an Iroh-to-localhost bridge.
- Exact Iroh fields for the setup-code payload.
- Whether paired client records should store and enforce client Iroh `EndpointId`s.
- Which Iroh endpoint configuration fields should be exposed 1:1 under `gateway.iroh.endpoint`.
- Whether browser/WASM support should be required for the first experiment or left as future work.

## RFC lifecycle

- Status: draft
- Maintainer discussion thread: TODO
- Implementation issue: created only if accepted
target.author: bjesuiter
